sourceScope.sourceReference: New York State Department of Financial Services proposed Sixth Amendment to 11 NYCRR 103 (Insurance Regulation 213)
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: The file is a proposed amendment text, not final adopted rule text.
sourceScope.rawSourceRoot: C:\Dev\NAIC Valuation Manual Course\NAIC Valuation Regulation

observedSectionWindows.note: Proposed Sixth Amendment text and footnote revision.

boundaries: Keep the proposed amendment separate from the core Regulation 213 wave.
Keep the FAQ addendum and the other amendment-history PDFs in the same folder out of this wave unless a later amendment-history plan explicitly opens them.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the text layer to remain page-image-backed for exact wording.
Keep the one-page window narrow so the boundary logic stays easy to review.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No Regulation 213 core-wave extraction in this batch.
No FAQ addendum pages in this wave.
No other amendment-history PDFs in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Proposed Sixth Amendment text and footnote revision
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the proposed amendment title and footnote revision together and stop at the end of the page.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 213 Sixth Amendment proposed text and footnote revision

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the proposed amendment title, adoption framing, and the footnote revision as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the proposed amendment title, adoption framing, and the footnote revision as one narrow review-only slice.

reviewStandards.proposedAmendment: Treat the source as proposed regulatory text and not as final adopted rule text unless later confirmation says otherwise.
reviewStandards.stateRegulation: Treat the source as New York regulatory material tied to Regulation 213.
reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat direct references to regulatory duties as mapped regulatory context, but do not promote the proposed amendment itself into binding text.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat incorporated-manual and footnote language as documentation context if it appears.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Valuation Manual and the amended footnote as mapped context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title and introductory text as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or ambiguous amendment wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

validationRequirements: The plan JSON must exist and parse cleanly.
The plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The dedicated batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
The batch must stay review-only by default.
The source must remain proposed and not promoted.
The source must stay separate from the core Regulation 213 wave and the FAQ addendum.
The source should remain page-image-backed for exact wording where needed.
